name: creatable-naming
description: Vibe test comparing prop names for XDSTokenizer free-text token creation: isCreatable vs hasCreate vs hasAddNew

prompts.prompt: Build a blog post editor form with a title field, body textarea, and a tag input where users can type custom tags and add them. Tags don't come from a predefined list — users create whatever tags they want.
prompts.tests: Basic free-text token creation, no search source needed
prompts.notes: Core use case — does the LLM discover and use the creatable prop for a pure free-text tag input?

prompts.prompt: Create a project settings page with a 'Labels' section. Users should be able to search from existing labels (Bug, Feature, Enhancement, Documentation, Question) but also type and create new custom labels that don't exist yet.
prompts.tests: Hybrid: search source + creatable mode together
prompts.notes: Tests whether the LLM combines searchSource with the creatable prop correctly

prompts.prompt: Build an invite flow where users type email addresses to invite team members. Each email becomes a token chip. Users should be able to type any email, not just pick from a list. Show validation that emails are properly formatted.
prompts.notes: Tests whether the LLM naturally reaches for creatable when building an email input

prompts.prompt: Create a search filter component for a log viewer. Users can add multiple search terms as token chips — each term is free text that they type. Include a clear-all button.
prompts.tests: Free-text tokens in a non-tag context (search terms)
prompts.notes: Tests whether 'creatable' naming generalizes beyond the tag metaphor

prompts.prompt: Build a recipe ingredient entry. Users type ingredient names (like 'flour', 'sugar', 'vanilla extract') and each one becomes a chip. The list is open-ended — any ingredient name is valid. Show a count of total ingredients added.
prompts.tests: Domain-specific free-text, count display alongside tokenizer
prompts.notes: Tests creatable in a domain where no search source makes sense

prompts.prompt: Build a skills selector for a job posting form. Provide a search source with common skills (React, TypeScript, Python, Java, CSS, SQL, Docker, AWS, Node.js, GraphQL) but let hiring managers also type and add niche skills that aren't in the predefined list.
prompts.tests: Hybrid with onChange distinguishing created vs selected
prompts.notes: Tests whether the LLM uses the change.type to distinguish 'add' vs 'create'

prompts.prompt: Create a CSV column mapper. Show a tokenizer for each target field where users can either search from detected CSV headers (Name, Email, Phone, Address, City, State, Zip) or type a custom column name if the CSV has different headers.
prompts.tests: Multiple tokenizers with maxEntries=1 and creatable
prompts.notes: Tests creatable with maxEntries constraint — single-select creatable typeahead pattern

prompts.prompt: Build a notification rules editor. Users configure alert recipients by typing Slack channel names (like #engineering, #incidents, #on-call). Channels are free-text — no predefined list. Each channel becomes a removable chip. Limit to 5 channels max.
